response.justification: The candidate is a strong, experienced generalist engineer (~15 years) with notable strengths in low-latency, event-driven, and concurrent systems, plus solid testing/CI-CD discipline. However, this role is fundamentally C++-centric, and the resume shows only a brief, dated stint of C/C++ work (~6 months at National Instruments in 2015) and a university thesis. The bulk of the career is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of the core domain requirements: IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, or industrial automation. Strong adjacent skills do not offset the missing C++ depth and industrial-cybersecurity background central to this position.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput, concurrent systems experience (millions of requests/sec)
Event-driven and distributed architecture exposure via GISCollective and microservices work
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring/teaching
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2015); core stack is TS/Node/DLang
No industrial cybersecurity background — no IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, Modbus, or OPC UA
No industrial automation or IoT platform domain experience relevant to the role
